author: marcinbugaj_94168
content:
Hello,

I’m trying to better understand your RISC-V .NET toolchain setup and was wondering if you could provide a full and complete reference for your runtime and compiler modifications.

Specifically, is the implementation primarily located in the following repositories?

* [dotnet-riscv](https://github.com/NethermindEth/dotnet-riscv)
* [bflat-riscv64](https://github.com/NethermindEth/bflat-riscv64)

If there are additional patches, forks, or internal changes beyond these repos, it would be very helpful to know where the authoritative or most up-to-date version of the runtime and compiler modifications can be found.

Thanks in advance for your help.

author: maximmenshikov_
content:
<@1408119112743256134> ir tends to be a good overview of changes.
Regarding ere, we have revamped support for Nethermind guest here:

github.com/nethermindeth/ere-guests

minus that it is bound not to master at this point (re <@774033563732541451> I saw your changes in ere-guests too)

We have also worked on workload updates. They are in our fork, but since we had major changes in the the guest, it needs to be updated before I claim it is ready.

author: maximmenshikov_
content: Yes. It is the main patch to improve operation with bare metal riscv

author: marcinbugaj_94168
content:
So two repositories:
dotnet-riscv
bflat-riscv64

Are there any other repositories that modify compiler / runtime?

author: maximmenshikov_
content:
https://github.com/NethermindEth/riscv-alpine-build
this repo simply constructs build environment so that .NET runtime doesn’t struggle. In fact it is mostly unused besides musl (which comes unaltered)

But that’s all
